functions.name: ClearRegistration
functions.description: Clears registration details stored with StoreRegistration . Added Windows support in plugin version 4.4. You may also need to call Unregister function if needed.

examples.label: Clears registration:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ClearRegistration" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Remove license in closing script for SBA solution:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsRegistered") ]
If [
MBS
( "
Plugin.LicenseeName
" ) = "MyName" ]
# forget license now
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Unregister" ) ]
# and clear from preferences file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ClearRegistration" ) ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("IsRegistered") ] 
	If [ MBS( "Plugin.LicenseeName" ) = "MyName" ] 
		# forget license now
		Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "Unregister" ) ] 
		# and clear from preferences file
		Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "ClearRegistration" ) ] 

	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.Completed
functions.description: Queries completed state. A Boolean value that states whether the player has completed the achievement. The value of this property is 1 if the percentComplete property is equal to 100.0; otherwise, it is 0.

parameters.name: AchievementRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement.

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.GetPercentComplete
functions.description: Queries percent complete. A percentage value that states how far the player has progressed on this achievement. The default value for a newly initialized achievement object is 0.0. The range of legal values is between 0.0 and 100.0, inclusive. You decide how that percentage is calculated and when it changes. For example, if the player earns an achievement simply for discovering a location in your game, then you would simply report the achievement as 100 percent complete the first time you report progress to Game Center. On the other hand, for an achievement like "Capture 10 pirates", your reporting mechanism increments by 10 percent each time the player captures a pirate.

parameters.name: AchievementRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement.

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.GetShowsCompletionBanner
functions.description: Queries whether to show completion banner. A Boolean value that states whether a banner is displayed when the achievement is completed. When an achievement is completed and the value of this property is 1, a notification banner is displayed to the player to inform them of the completed achievement. If the value of this property is 0, there is no visual indication that the achievement is completed. Your game should set this property to 0 to provide its own visual indicator that the achievement was earned. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: AchievementRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement.

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.LastReportedDate
functions.description: Queries last reported time. The last time that progress on the achievement was successfully reported to Game Center. On a newly initialized achievement object, this property holds the current date.

parameters.name: AchievementRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement.

examples.label: Query last reported date:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GameKit.Achievement.LastReportedDate";  "de.mbsplugins.test")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.New
functions.description: Initializes a new achievement object for the local player. Your game initializes a new achievement object only when it has not previously reported progress for that achievement. If your game has previously reported progress on an achievement, you should first retrieve the current progress by calling the GameKit.LoadAchievements function. The identifier string should be a reverse DNS style string, such as "com.mycompany.myapplication.myactivity". Zero-length strings are not allowed.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the archiement.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The identifier for the player.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GameKit.Achievement.SetPercentComplete
functions.description: Sets percent complete. A percentage value that states how far the player has progressed on this achievement. The default value for a newly initialized achievement object is 0.0. The range of legal values is between 0.0 and 100.0, inclusive. You decide how that percentage is calculated and when it changes. For example, if the player earns an achievement simply for discovering a location in your game, then you would simply report the achievement as 100 percent complete the first time you report progress to Game Center. On the other hand, for an achievement like "Capture 10 pirates", your reporting mechanism increments by 10 percent each time the player captures a pirate.

parameters.name: AchievementRef

parameters.name: Value

When an achievement is completed and the value of this property is 1, a notification banner is displayed to the player to inform them of the completed achievement. If the value of this property is 0, there is no visual indication that the achievement is completed. Your game should set this property to 0 to provide its own visual indicator that the achievement was earned. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: GameKit.AchievementDescription.Hidden
functions.description: Queries hidden flag. A Boolean value that states whether this achievement is initially visible to players. If the value of this property is 0, this achievement is always visible to the player. If 1, the achievement is not displayed in any of the standard achievement user interface screens. It remains hidden until the first time your game reports progress towards completing this achievement.

parameters.name: AchievementDescriptionRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement description.

functions.name: GameKit.AchievementDescription.Image
functions.description: Loads the image property for a completed achievement. Loading and displaying achievements using a custom interface is a two-step process, with the first step being to call the GameKit.LoadAchievementDescriptions function. The second step is to call the GameKit.AchievementDescription.Image function for each achievement the user has completed. Your game should display the placeholder image until the image is successfully downloaded.

parameters.name: AchievementDescriptionRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement description.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: For PDF or image files returned an optional file name.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: For images the type you like to get. Can be JPEG, TIFF, PNG, GIF, BMP or PDF.

functions.name: GameKit.AchievementDescription.Replayable
functions.description: Queries whether achievement is replay-able. A Boolean value that states whether this achievement can be earned multiple times. If the value of this property is 0, then the achievement may only be earned once. After the achievement is earned, Game Center ignores any further progress submitted for it. If the value of this property is 1, then the achievement is considered earned each time your game reports progress to Game Center that completes the achievement. This means that any appropriate banners are displayed to the player again, challenges based on the achievement are completed, and so on.

parameters.name: AchievementDescriptionRef
parameters.description: The identifier for the achievement description.

functions.name: GameKit.DefaultLeaderboardIdentifier
functions.description: Loads the category identifier for the local player’s default leaderboard. Returns the category ID string for the local player’s default leaderboard.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.Dismiss
functions.description: Dismisses the dialog.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.GetLeaderboardIdentifier
functions.description: Queries leaderboard. The named leaderboard that is displayed by the view controller.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.GetLeaderboardTimeScope
functions.description: Queries time scope. A filter used to restrict the search to scores that were posted within a specific period of time. Can be "Week", "Today" or "AllTime".

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.GetViewState
functions.description: Queries view state. The content displayed by the Game Center controller. When you first present the Game Center view controller, the content displayed by the view controller is determined by this property. If the player navigates to different content, the view state is automatically updated. For example, to preserve the player’s selections, you can read the viewState property after the screen is dismissed, and set that value the next time you initialize the view controller. Value can be Default, Leaderboards, Achievements or Challenges.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes Game Center view. The Game Center view provides a single user interface used to display achievements and leaderboards supported by Game Center.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.Present
functions.description: Presents the dialog. For iOS this is full screen and for MacOS on the current window.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.SetLeaderboardIdentifier
functions.description: Sets leaderboard. The named leaderboard that is displayed by the view controller. The leaderboardIdentifier property must either be empty or it must match a leaderboard identifier you defined when you created your leaderboards in App Store Connect. If empty, the view displays scores for the aggregate leaderboard. Default is empty. When the leaderboard is presented, the value of this property determines which leaderboard content is displayed to the player. As the player changes which leaderboard content they view, the leaderboardIdentifier property is automatically updated. For example, to preserve the player’s selections, you can read the leaderboardIdentifier property after the screen is dismissed, and set that value the next time you initialize the view controller.

parameters.name: LeaderboardIdentifier
parameters.description: The leaderboard identifier for the leaderboard to use.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.SetLeaderboardTimeScope
functions.description: Sets time scope. A filter used to restrict the search to scores that were showed within a specific period of time. Can be "Week", "Today" or "AllTime". This property determines which tab view of the scores screen is displayed to the player. The default value is AllTime, which shows the best score each player has earned. For more information on time scopes, see Leaderboard functions. When the leaderboard is presented, the value of this property determines the initial tab that is displayed to the player. As the player changes which tab they view, the leaderboardTimeScope property is automatically updated. For example, to preserve the player’s selections, you can read the leaderboardTimeScope property after the screen is dismissed, and set that value the next time you initialize the view controller.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.description: The new time scope. Can be Today, Week or AllTime.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.SetScriptTrigger
functions.description: Sets script trigger to call when dialog finishes. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details. This function is free to use. Created 5th November 2018 , last changed 13th November 2021 GameKit.GameCenterView.SetLeaderboardTimeScope - GameKit.GameCenterView.SetViewState 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.SetViewState
functions.description: Sets the view state. The content displayed by the Game Center controller. When you first present the Game Center view controller, the content displayed by the view controller is determined by this property. If the player navigates to different content, the view state is automatically updated. For example, to preserve the player’s selections, you can read the viewState property after the screen is dismissed, and set that value the next time you initialize the view controller. Value can be Default, Leaderboards, Achievements or Challenges.

parameters.name: ViewState
parameters.description: The view state to use. Can be Leaderboards, Achievements, Challenges or Default.

functions.name: GameKit.GameCenterView.ShowBannerWithTitle
functions.description: Displays a banner to the player. Optional for a specified period of time. This function is free to use. Created 5th November 2018 , last changed 8th November 2018 GameKit.GameCenterView.SetViewState - GameKit.Initialize 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title for the banner.

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: A secondary message to be displayed.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: The amount of time that the banner should be displayed to the player. In seconds.

functions.name: GameKit.Initialize
functions.description: Initialized GameKit. Loads the framework, setups script triggers and loads local player. Must be called once before doing any other method.

examples.label: Initialize GameKit:
examples.text_code:
# Try to init GameKit
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GameKit.Initialize" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to initialize GameKit." ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# Set script to call when player changes
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GameKit.SetPlayerDidChangeScriptTrigger
"; Get(FileName); "PlayerChanged" ) ]
# Login if needed
If [
MBS
( "
GameKit.LocalPlayer.isAuthenticated
" ) = 0 ]
# Show Login dialog
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GameKit.LocalPlayer.Authenticate
" ) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Try to init GameKit</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GameKit.Initialize" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to initialize GameKit." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set script to call when player changes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GameKit.SetPlayerDidChangeScriptTrigger"; Get(FileName); "PlayerChanged" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Login if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS( "GameKit.LocalPlayer.isAuthenticated" ) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show Login dialog</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GameKit.LocalPlayer.Authenticate" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

The playerScope property is ignored if the leaderboard request was initialized using the player identifiers list. Otherwise, the playerScope property determines which players are included in the request for high scores. The default is Global. Value can be FriendsOnly or Global.

functions.name: GameKit.Leaderboard.New
functions.description: Creates new leaderboard. If player list is given, returns an identifier for the leaderboard object initialized with this method ignores the playerScope and range properties. Instead, it retrieves scores for the specific list of players whose player identifiers are included in the players parameter. If no player list is given, a leaderboard object initialized with this method uses the playerScope, timeScope, and range properties to search Game Center for scores.

This property is invalid until a call to GameKit.Leaderboard.LoadScores is completed. Afterward, it contains the same score objects that were returned to the completion handler.

functions.name: GameKit.Leaderboard.SetRange
functions.description: Sets the numerical score rankings to return from the search. The range property is ignored if the leaderboard request was initialized using the player list option. Otherwise, the range property is used to filter which scores are returned to your game. For example, if you specified a range of [1,10], after the search is complete, your game receives the best ten scores. The default range is [1,25]. The minimum index is 1. The maximum length is 100.

functions.name: GameKit.LocalPlayer
functions.description: Retrieves the shared instance of the local player. You never directly create a local player object. Instead, you retrieve the Singleton object by calling this function. Identifier changes when authenticated, but the plugin can always use local player if you pass "localplayer" as player identifier.

functions.name: GameKit.LocalPlayer.Authenticate
functions.description: Authenticates the local user. Your game should authenticate the player as early as possible after launching, ideally as soon as you can present a user interface to the player. For example, your game may be launched because the player accepted an invitation to join a match or to take a turn in a turn-based match, so you want your game to authenticate the player and process the match invitation as quickly as possible. After you set a handler, authentication begins automatically and is repeated when your game moves to the background and then back to the foreground. Check later status via GameKit.LocalPlayer.isAuthenticated and GameKit.LocalPlayer.AuthenticateError functions.

examples.label: Login if needed:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
GameKit.LocalPlayer.isAuthenticated
" ) = 0 ]
# Show Login dialog
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GameKit.LocalPlayer.Authenticate" ) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "GameKit.LocalPlayer.isAuthenticated" ) = 0 ] 
	# Show Login dialog
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "GameKit.LocalPlayer.Authenticate" ) ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GameKit.Score.FormattedValue
functions.description: Returns the player’s score as a localized string. This property is invalid on a newly initialized score object. On a score returned from GameKit, it contains a formatted string based on the player’s score. You determine how a score is formatted when you define the leaderboard in App Store Connect. Never convert the value property into a string directly; always use this method to receive the formatted string. This function is free to use. Created 5th November 2018 , last changed 8th November 2018 GameKit.Score.Date - GameKit.Score.GetContext 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GameKit.Score.GetContext
functions.description: Queries context. An integer value used by your game. The context property is stored and returned to your game, but is otherwise ignored by Game Center. It allows your game to associate an arbitrary 64-bit unsigned integer value with the score data reported to Game Center. You decide how this integer value is interpreted by your game. For example, you might use the context property to store flags that provide game-specific details about a player’s score, or you might use the context as a key to other data stored on the device or on your own server. The context is most useful when your game displays a custom leaderboard user interface.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.GetShouldSetDefaultLeaderboard
functions.description: Queries whether this score should update default leaderboard. A Boolean value that indicates whether this score should also update the default leaderboard. If the value of this property is true, when the score is reported to Game Center, Game Center also updates the local player’s default leaderboard to match the value stored in the leaderboardIdentifier property of the score object. If the value of this property is false, the default leaderboard is not changed by reporting the score. The default value of this property is false.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.GetValue
functions.description: Queries score value. The score earned by the player. You can use any algorithm you want to calculate scores in your game. Your game must set the value property before reporting a score, otherwise an error is returned. The value provided by a score object is interpreted by Game Center only when formatted for display. You determine how your scores are formatted when you define the leaderboard in App Store Connect.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.LeaderboardIdentifier

functions.description: Creates a new score object. Returns score identifier for a new score object for the specified leaderboard and optional player. Your game explicitly allocates and initializes a score object using the designated player and current date when it needs to report a new score to Game Center.

parameters.name: LeaderboardIdentifier

parameters.name: PlayerIdentifier

functions.name: GameKit.Score.Player
functions.description: Queries the player identifier for the player that earned the score. When you initialize a new score object, the player property is set to the identifier for the local player. If the score object was returned to your game by loading scores from Game Center, the player property identifies the player who recorded that score.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.Rank
functions.description: Queries position of the score in the results of a leaderboard search. The value of this property is only valid on score objects returned from Game Center. The rank property represents the position of the score in the returned results, with 1 being the best score, 2 being the second best, and so on.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.SetContext
functions.description: Sets context value. An integer value used by your game. The context property is stored and returned to your game, but is otherwise ignored by Game Center. It allows your game to associate an arbitrary 64-bit unsigned integer value with the score data reported to Game Center. You decide how this integer value is interpreted by your game. For example, you might use the context property to store flags that provide game-specific details about a player’s score, or you might use the context as a key to other data stored on the device or on your own server. The context is most useful when your game displays a custom leaderboard user interface.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the score object.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new context value. This is a 64-bit number you can store with the score.

functions.name: GameKit.Score.SetShouldSetDefaultLeaderboard
functions.description: Set whether this score should update default leaderboard. A Boolean value that indicates whether this score should also update the default leaderboard. If the value of this property is true, when the score is reported to Game Center, Game Center also updates the local player’s default leaderboard to match the value stored in the leaderboardIdentifier property of the score object. If the value of this property is false, the default leaderboard is not changed by reporting the score. The default value of this property is false.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: GameKit.Score.SetValue
functions.description: Sets score value. The score earned by the player. You can use any algorithm you want to calculate scores in your game. Your game must set the value property before reporting a score, otherwise an error is returned. The value provided by a score object is interpreted by Game Center only when formatted for display. You determine how your scores are formatted when you define the leaderboard in App Store Connect.

parameters.name: ScoreIdentifier

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new score value.

functions.name: Hash.CRC16
functions.signature: MBS( "Hash.CRC16"; InputType; InputData { ; InputEncoding; StartValue; Poly; FinalXOR; ReflectInput; ReflectOutput } )
functions.description: Calculates 16bit CRC value. You can fill the parameters to get very different CRC variations.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: StartValue
parameters.description: The start value for the CRC. Default is 65535.

parameters.name: Poly
parameters.description: The polynom to use. Default is 4129 = hex 1021 e.g. * hex 3D65 for DNP, IEC 870, M-BUS, wM-BUS and others * hex 1021 for CCITT, X.25, V.41, HDLC FCS, Bluetooth and others * hex 8005 for IBM, ModBus, USB, Bisync, CRC-16, CRC-16-ANSI and others * hex 8BB7 for SCSI DIF * hex 0589 for DECT Cordeless Telephones * hex A02B for ACARS Aplications

parameters.name: FinalXOR
parameters.description: The final XOR. Pass 0 to skip.

parameters.name: ReflectInput
parameters.description: Whether to flip input bits. Default is 0 to no flip.

parameters.name: ReflectOutput
parameters.description: Whether to flip output bits. Default is 0 to no flip.

examples.label: Try CRC with CCITT and 0xFFFF as start value:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
r =
MBS
("Hash.CRC16"; "Text"; "123456789"; "UTF-8"; 65535; 4129);
h =
MBS
("
Math.DecToHex
"; r)
]; r & " " & h)
Example result:
10673 000029B1

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
  r = MBS("Hash.CRC16"; "Text"; "123456789"; "UTF-8"; 65535; 4129);
  h = MBS("Math.DecToHex"; r)
]; r & " " & h)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try CRC with MODBUS:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
r =
MBS
("Hash.CRC16"; "Text"; "123456789"; "UTF-8"; 65535; 40961; 0; 1; 0);
h =
MBS
("
Math.DecToHex
"; r)
]; r & " " & h)
// poly is hex A001, which is reflected 8005
Example result:
15739 00003D7B

functions.name: Hash.Digest
functions.signature: MBS( "Hash.Digest"; Algorithm; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; OutputType { ; OutputInfo } )
functions.description: Calculates a hash of a value. Input can be one of different types: Text, you pass "text", your text and the text encoding. If no or invalid encoding name is passed, the native one is used. The encoding can be one of the following strings: ansi, windows, mac, latin1, utf8, dos or native. PDF, you pass the container with the PDF file inside. Image, you pass the container with an image file (JPEG, GIF, TIFF, PNG, PICT). Data, you pass container and plugin looks for the file data inside. Hex, you pass a hex encoded text. Base64, you pass a base64 encoded text. Path, you pass the file path to a file to read. Container, you pass a container which is packed with all its content. Now for output we can return the digest in various ways: Text, we return the hash as text in the encoding. This is not recommended for hash values. OutputInfo parameter is text encoding. Base64, the hash is returned as base64 encoded text. Hex, the hash is returned as hex encoded text. File, the hash is returned as a container value with a file inside containing the hash. OutputInfo parameter is the filename to be stored in the container value. Path, the hash is written to a file. In the OutputInfo parameter you provide a native file path. JPEG, a container with a JPEG image. GIF, a container with a GIF image. TIFF, a container with a TIFF image. PNG, a container with a PNG image. Possible digest engines: DSA-SHA, DSA, MD5, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA, SHA1, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input. Can be Text, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path. Added Base64URL as option for plugin version 10.3.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

examples.label: Stream file to AWS:
examples.text_code:
# Build a path to file (local) and in AWS
Set Variable [
$Filename
; Value: "test.pdf" ]
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value: "/" &
$BucketName
& "/PDF/" &
$Filename
]
Set Variable [
$localPath
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/license.pdf" ]
# calculate hash for file to upload
Set Variable [
$HashedPayload
; Value: Lower(
MBS
( "Hash.Digest"; "sha256"; "path";
$localPath
; ""; "hex" )) ]
# upload
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
# file content is streamed
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.OpenInputFile
";
$curl
;
$localPath
) ]
# setup AWS
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetupAWS
";
$curl
;
$AWSAccessKeyId
;
$AWSSecretAccessKey
;
$Region
;
$Service
;
$Path
;
$Domain
;
$Verb
;
$HashedPayload
;
$headers
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build a path to file (local) and in AWS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"test.pdf" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/" & $BucketName & "/PDF/" & $Filename </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/license.pdf" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$localPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate hash for file to upload</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Lower(MBS( "Hash.Digest"; "sha256"; "path"; $localPath; ""; "hex" )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$HashedPayload</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> upload</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> file content is streamed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.OpenInputFile"; $curl; $localPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> setup AWS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetupAWS"; $curl; $AWSAccessKeyId; $AWSSecretAccessKey; $Region; $Service; $Path; $Domain; $Verb; $HashedPayload; $headers) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="debug "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate x5t for a certificate file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
// read PEM file
cert = MBS( "X509.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Downloads/Cert.pem" );
// write DER file
data = MBS( "X509.Write"; cert; 1 );
// and free memory
r = MBS( "X509.Release"; cert );
// hash it to get x5t
hash = MBS( "Hash.Digest"; "SHA1"; "Base64"; data; ""; "base64url")
]; hash )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Hash.DigestFile
functions.signature: MBS( "Hash.DigestFile"; Algorithm; FilePath; OutputType { ; OutputInfo; UpdateProgressbar } )
functions.description: Calculates a hash of a file. This function is optimized to process files on disk in chunks, so it works well for several GB of file sizes. For output we can return the digest in various ways: Base64, the hash is returned as base64 encoded text. Hex, the hash is returned as hex encoded text. Possible digest engines: DSA-SHA, DSA, MD5, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA, SHA1, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path to use.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path. Added Base64URL as option for plugin version 10.3.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

parameters.name: UpdateProgressbar
parameters.description: Pass 1 to keep a progressbar updated for the digest in progress. Only useful for bigger files.

functions.name: Hash.MD5
functions.description: Calculated MD5 hash of given text. Text is converted to UTF-8 to make sure the text encoding doesn't change the hash value. Hashes are used to store a fingerprint of some data. When hashes are not equal, the data is probably also not equal. This function is good to make hashes for passwords. You don't save the password in your database, but only the hash. On login, you calculate the hash for the password entered by the user and compare the hash with the stored one. This way the database only contains hashes. And it's unlikely someone finds the password given the hash, except for easy cases like "hello" where google will tell you the text for the hash. To make hashes more secure, use a prefix/suffix like the user id or simply your application name. This way even hello as a password will be quite impossible to recover from the hash. SHA-512 is stronger than MD5. Please be aware that text can result in different hashes if it contains line endings. You may want to make sure that line endings are right with the Text.ReplaceNewLine function before you compare hashes to results in other development tools. FileMaker uses Mac line endings by default and most examples for Javascript use Unix line endings.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if input data is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 8 for base64URL encoding. (new in v10.3)

examples.label: Calculate hash of password:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.MD5"; $password )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

( "Hash.MD5"; "48656C6C6F"; 2 )]

$hash1
& ¶ &
$hash2
]

functions.name: Hash.MD5.HMAC
functions.description: Returns the a MD5 HMAC based on the key and the data string. Keyed-Hash Message Authentication Code is a way to add salt to a hash for more security. Please be aware that text can result in different hashes if it contains line endings. You may want to make sure that line endings are right with the Text.ReplaceNewLine function before you compare hashes to results in other development tools. FileMaker uses Mac line endings by default and most examples for Javascript use Unix line endings.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key text to use.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The data text to use.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if key is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 4 if data is hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 8 for base64URL encoding. (new in v10.3)

examples.label: Hash empty string
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.MD5.HMAC"; ""; "" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Hash.PBKDF2.HMAC
functions.signature: MBS( "Hash.PBKDF2.HMAC"; salt; Password; iterations; desiredHashLength; hashAlgorithm { ; Flags } )
functions.description: Returns the PBKDF2 hash value of the data, first applying the salt value and using the specified hashAlgorithm. Same as Crypto.PBKDF2 in Xojo (Real Studio): http://docs.realsoftware.com/index.php/Crypto.PBKDF2

parameters.name: salt
parameters.description: The key text to use.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The data text to use.

parameters.name: iterations
parameters.description: Number of loops that the hash algorithm does.

parameters.name: desiredHashLength
parameters.description: Number of bytes that you want the resulting hash to be. 16 or 32 bytes are commonly used.

parameters.name: hashAlgorithm
parameters.description: The hash algorithm. Can be MD5, SHA1, SHA256 or SHA512.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if salt is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 4 if password is hex encoded and plugin should decode it first.

examples.label: Calculate password hash:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.PBKDF2.HMAC"; "Hello"; "World"; 100; 32; "SHA512")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Hash.SHA1
functions.description: Calculated SHA-1 hash of given text. Text is converted to UTF-8 to make sure the text encoding doesn't change the hash value. Hashes are used to store a fingerprint of some data. When hashes are not equal, the data is probably also not equal. This function is good to make hashes for passwords. You don't save the password in your database, but only the hash. On login, you calculate the hash for the password entered by the user and compare the hash with the stored one. This way the database only contains hashes. And it's unlikely someone finds the password given the hash, except for easy cases like "hello" where google will tell you the text for the hash. To make hashes more secure, use a prefix/suffix like the user id or simply your application name. This way even hello as a password will be quite impossible to recover from the hash. SHA-1 is stronger than MD5 and weaker than SHA-512. Please be aware that text can result in different hashes if it contains line endings. You may want to make sure that line endings are right with the Text.ReplaceNewLine function before you compare hashes to results in other development tools. FileMaker uses Mac line endings by default and most examples for Javascript use Unix line endings.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if input data is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 8 for base64URL encoding. (new in v10.3)

examples.label: Calculate hash of password:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.SHA1"; $password )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Encode Password like in PHP:
examples.text_code:
# original in PHP:
#
#
$nonce_date_pwd
= pack("A*",
$nonce
) . pack("A*",
$CREATIONDATE
) . pack("H*", sha1(
$password
));
#
$PASSWORDDIGEST
= base64_encode( pack('H*', sha1(
$nonce_date_pwd
)));
#
# in FileMaker:
#
# you can use
Hash.RandomHexString
to make your Nonce.
#
Set Variable [
$NonceHexEncoded
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.EncodeToHex
";
Password Digest::Nonce
) ]
Set Variable [
$CreationDateHexEncoded
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.EncodeToHex
";
Password Digest::CreationDate
) ]
Set Variable [
$PasswordHashHexEncoded
; Value:
MBS
( "Hash.SHA1";
Password Digest::Password
; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$Input
; Value:
$NonceHexEncoded
&
$CreationDateHexEncoded
&
$PasswordHashHexEncoded
]
# build hash by decoding from Hex, doing SHA1 and encode back to Base64
Set Variable [
$PasswordDigest
; Value:
MBS
( "
Hash.Digest
"; "SHA1"; "hex";
$input
; ""; "Base64"; "" ) ]
Set Field [
Password Digest::PasswordDigest
;
$PasswordDigest
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> original in PHP:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> $nonce_date_pwd = pack("A*",$nonce) . pack("A*",$CREATIONDATE) . pack("H*", sha1($password));</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> $PASSWORDDIGEST = base64_encode( pack('H*', sha1($nonce_date_pwd)));</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> in FileMaker:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> you can use Hash.RandomHexString to make your Nonce.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.EncodeToHex"; Password Digest::Nonce) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NonceHexEncoded</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.EncodeToHex"; Password Digest::CreationDate) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CreationDateHexEncoded</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.SHA1"; Password Digest::Password; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PasswordHashHexEncoded</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$NonceHexEncoded & $CreationDateHexEncoded & $PasswordHashHexEncoded </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Input</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build hash by decoding from Hex, doing SHA1 and encode back to Base64</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.Digest"; "SHA1"; "hex"; $input; ""; "Base64"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PasswordDigest</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PasswordDigest </Calculation>
    <Field table="Password Digest" id="99999" name="PasswordDigest "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Hash.SHA256.HMAC
functions.description: Returns the a SHA256 HMAC based on the key and the data string. Keyed-Hash Message Authentication Code is a way to add salt to a hash for more security. By default we return the hash as hex encoded string. Please be aware that text can result in different hashes if it contains line endings. You may want to make sure that line endings are right with the Text.ReplaceNewLine function before you compare hashes to results in other development tools. FileMaker uses Mac line endings by default and most examples for Javascript use Unix line endings. Can be used to sign requests for Amazon MWS.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key text to use.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The data text to use.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if key is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 4 if data is hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 8 for base64URL encoding. (new in v10.3)

examples.label: Calculate Amazon AWS Signature for canonical query:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Verb
; Value: "POST" ]
Set Variable [
$Domain
; Value: "mws.amazonservices.com" ]
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value: "/Orders/2013-09-01" ]
Set Variable [
$QueryString
; Value: "AWSAccessKeyId=AKIAJVZ5JFVD2SERTQIA&Action=GetOrder&AmazonOrderId.Id
.1
=112-4707920-3733828&SellerId=A1DGCWQ1HE6UN1&SignatureMethod=HmacSHA256&SignatureVersion=2&Timestamp=2018-01-24T22%3A34%3A24Z&Version=2013-09-01" ]
Set Variable [
$SecretKey
; Value: "1234567890" ]
Set Variable [
$Signature
; Value:
MBS
( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC";
$SecretKey
;
$Verb
& Char(10) &
$Domain
& Char(10) &
$Path
& Char(10) &
$QueryString
; 1 ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "AWS Signature" ;
$signature
]
Example result:
“hTX0IGOH+lWyaqZBf513TcB6Mk3U/hidyQvJASsn7aE="
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"POST" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Verb</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"mws.amazonservices.com" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Domain</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Orders/2013-09-01" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"AWSAccessKeyId=AKIAJVZ5JFVD2SERTQIA&Action=GetOrder&AmazonOrderId.Id.1=112-4707920-3733828&SellerId=A1DGCWQ1HE6UN1&SignatureMethod=HmacSHA256&SignatureVersion=2&Timestamp=2018-01-24T22%3A34%3A24Z&Version=2013-09-01" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$QueryString</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"1234567890" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SecretKey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC"; $SecretKey; $Verb & Char(10) & $Domain & Char(10) & $Path & Char(10) & $QueryString; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Signature</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "AWS Signature" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$signature </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate a JSON Web Token (JWT) in FileMaker:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [

// Header: {\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}
// Payload: {"iss":"scotch.io","exp":1300819380,"name":"Chris Sevilleja","admin":true}

// expected result: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJsb2dnZWRJbkFzIjoiYWRtaW4iLCJpYXQiOjE0MjI3Nzk2Mzh9.gzSraSYS8EXBxLN_oWnFSRgCzcmJmMjLiuyu5CSpyHI

secret = "secretkey";
header = "{\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}";
payload = "{\"loggedInAs\":\"admin\",\"iat\":1422779638}";
encodedString = MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; header; "UTF-8") & "." & MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; payload; "UTF-8");
// calculate hash
hash = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC"; secret; encodedString; 1+8 );
// and built final result:
result = encodedString & "." & hash
]; result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate the signature for AWS Signature Version 4:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 

// see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-calculate-signature.html

date="20150830";
Region = "us-east-1";
Service= "iam";
Signing= "aws4_request";

kSecret  = "wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG+bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY";
kDate = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC"; "AWS4" & kSecret ; date);
kRegion = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC”; kDate ; Region; 2);
kService = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC”; kRegion ; Service; 2);
kSigning = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC”; kService ; Signing; 2)

]; Lower (kSigning))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate hash for kaufland API:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
method = "POST";
uri = "https://sellerapi.kaufland.com/v2/units/";
body = "";
timestamp = 1411055926;
secretKey = "a7d0cb1da1ddbc86c96ee5fedd341b7d8ebfbb2f5c83cfe0909f4e57f05dd403";
texts = method & Char(10) & uri & Char(10) & body & Char(10) & timestamp;
result = MBS( "Hash.SHA256.HMAC"; secretKey; texts; 0)
]; lower(result) )
// see https://sellerapi.kaufland.com/?page=rest-api#signrequest-php</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Hash.SHA512
functions.description: Calculated SHA-512 hash of given text. Text is converted to UTF-8 to make sure the text encoding doesn't change the hash value. Hashes are used to store a fingerprint of some data. When hashes are not equal, the data is probably also not equal. This function is good to make hashes for passwords. You don't save the password in your database, but only the hash. On login, you calculate the hash for the password entered by the user and compare the hash with the stored one. This way the database only contains hashes. And it's unlikely someone finds the password given the hash, except for easy cases like "hello" where google will tell you the text for the hash. To make hashes more secure, use a prefix/suffix like the user id or simply your application name. This way even hello as a password will be quite impossible to recover from the hash. SHA-512 is stronger than MD5. Please be aware that text can result in different hashes if it contains line endings. You may want to make sure that line endings are right with the Text.ReplaceNewLine function before you compare hashes to results in other development tools. FileMaker uses Mac line endings by default and most examples for Javascript use Unix line endings.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags you can combine by addition. Pass 1 for getting result Base64 encoded instead of Hex encoded. Pass 2 if input data is Hex encoded and plugin should decode it first. Pass 8 for base64URL encoding. (new in v10.3)

examples.label: Calculate hash of password:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Hash.SHA512"; $password )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSApp.AddSwipeGestureRecognizer
functions.description: Add swipe gesture recognizers. Please make sure a layout is visible as we attach to the front layout. You may do a short pause after showing the layout for FileMaker to actually display it, before calling this function. This may replace a recognizer from FileMaker itself and it may prevent scrolling if we consume the gesture before FileMaker.

parameters.name: Directions
parameters.description: Pass directions you like to have. Can be Right, Left, Up and Down.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Install a recognizer:
examples.text_code:
# Let FileMaker make the layout visible
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds):
0.1
]
# and add our recognizers
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "iOSApp.AddSwipeGestureRecognizer"; "Right, Down, Up, Left"; Get(FileName); "Recognizer") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Let FileMaker make the layout visible</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>0.1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and add our recognizers</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSApp.AddSwipeGestureRecognizer"; "Right, Down, Up, Left"; Get(FileName); "Recognizer") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Queries the script name for the significant time change script trigger. See iOSApp.SetSignificantTimeChangeScript to set the script to trigger.
functions.result: Returns script name as text or error.

functions.name: iOSApp.GetWillEnterForegroundEvaluate
functions.description: Queries the expression to be evaluated when app goes to background.

functions.name: iOSApp.GetWillEnterForegroundScript
functions.description: Queries the script name for the will enter foreground script trigger. See iOSApp.SetWillEnterForegroundScript to set the script to trigger.
functions.result: Returns script name as text or error.

functions.name: iOSApp.GetWillResignActiveEvaluate
functions.description: Queries the expression to be evaluated when the app is no longer active and loses focus.

functions.name: iOSApp.GetWillResignActiveScript
functions.description: Queries the script name for the will resign active script trigger. See iOSApp.SetWillResignActiveScript to set the script to trigger.
functions.result: Returns script name as text or error.

functions.name: iOSApp.InstallOpenURLHandler
functions.description: Install OpenURL handler. Once installed, the MBS Plugin will be notified about new Open URL requests sent to your iOS application. We can trigger a script or evaluate an expression. We always store the URL for query via iOSApp.LastURL function.

examples.label: Installs URL open handler:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("iOSApp.InstallOpenURLHandler") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
iOSApp.SetDidOpenURLScript
"; Get(FileName); "URLArrived") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("iOSApp.InstallOpenURLHandler") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("iOSApp.SetDidOpenURLScript"; Get(FileName); "URLArrived") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSApp.IsOpenURLHandlerInstalled
functions.description: Returns whether OpenURL handler was installed. Value is 1 if iOSApp.InstallOpenURLHandler was called before.

functions.name: iOSApp.LastURL
functions.description: Queries last URL we received. You need to call iOSApp.InstallOpenURLHandler once to install the functionality. This may return whatever URL arrived, even if it is no fmp URL. You can call iOSApp.ClearLastURL to clear this value.

functions.name: iOSApp.ReadReceipt
functions.description: Reads and parses App Store receipt. Works for macOS and iOS receipt. Call without path in an iOS app from the iOS App Store. Or pass a path to test with an existing receipt.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The file path of the receipt to read. If empty, we read the receipt of current file.

examples.label: Read receipt of a macOS app:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("iOSApp.ReadReceipt"; "/Applications/Test.app/Contents/_MASReceipt/receipt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSApp.SetDidBecomeActiveEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to be evaluated when app becomes active. An app is active when it is receiving events. An active app can be said to have focus. It gains focus after being launched, loses focus when an overlay window pops up or when the device is locked, and gains focus when the device is unlocked. Version 10.2 and newer stores this in preferences, so it is restored if app when app starts again after being killed in background.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

functions.name: iOSApp.SetDidBecomeActiveScript
functions.description: Sets the script to be run when app becomes active. An app is active when it is receiving events. An active app can be said to have focus. It gains focus after being launched, loses focus when an overlay window pops up or when the device is locked, and gains focus when the device is unlocked. Version 10.2 and newer stores this in preferences, so it is restored if app when app starts again after being killed in background.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter.

examples.label: Install trigger:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSApp.SetDidBecomeActiveScript"; Get(FileName); "Trigger"; "DidBecomeActiveScript" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSApp.SetIdleTimerDisabled
functions.description: Sets whether the idle timer is disabled for the app. The default value of this property is 0. When most apps have no touches as user input for a short period, the system puts the device into a "sleep" state where the screen dims. This is done for the purposes of conserving power. However, apps that don't have user input except for the accelerometer-games, for instance-can, by setting this property to 1, disable the "idle timer" to avert system sleep. See also ProcessActivity.beginActivity function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value. Pass 1 to disable idle sleep or pass 0 to enable.

examples.label: Disable the idle timer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSApp.SetIdleTimerDisabled"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Enable the idle timer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSApp.SetIdleTimerDisabled"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSApp.SetOrientationDidChangeScript
functions.description: Queries the script to be run when device is rotated. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

      <Calculation>MBS("iOSApp.SetOrientationDidChangeScript"; Get(FileName); "OrientationDidChangeScript") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Log
functions.description: Writes the given values to the debug console. On Mac use Console.app in the Application Utilities folder. On Windows use DebugView application from Microsoft. On Windows with a FileMaker Server, you can see debug messages in DebugView if you run DebugView application as admin and enable global Win32 listening. For FileMaker Server on macOS you can see messages in /Library/FileMaker Server/Logs/stderr file. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Several parameters which are concated.

examples.label: Log a message:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Log"; "Test: "; $test ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Log last error:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Log"; "Lasterror: "; Get(LastError)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PKCS12.CA
functions.description: Queries certificate with given index. A PKCS12 file may contain more than one certificate.

parameters.name: PKCS12 Reference
parameters.description: The reference to the PKCS12.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the certificate to get. From 0 to PKCS12.CACount-1. Default 0.

functions.result: Returns X509 reference number or error.

examples.label: Query certificates:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$p12
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.ReadFromContainer";
test::Container
; "Geslo123#" )]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$p12
]
Else
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.CACount";
$p12
)]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:0]
Loop
Set Variable [
$cert
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.CA";
$p12
;
$index
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["Certificate";
MBS
( "X509.Description";
$cert
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.Release";
$cert
)]
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>=
$count
]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.Release";
$p12
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.ReadFromContainer"; test::Container; "Geslo123#" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p12</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$p12</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.CACount"; $p12 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.CA"; $p12; $index )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cert</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Certificate"; MBS( "X509.Description"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$cert )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "X509.Release"; $cert )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index >= $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.Release"; $p12 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PKCS12.ReadFromFile

Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Password
