functions.name: Window.FlashWindow
functions.description: Flashes the specified window one time. It does not change the active state of the window. The return value specifies the window's state before the call to the FlashWindow function. If the window caption was drawn as active before the call, the return value is nonzero. Otherwise, the return value is zero. Flashing a window means changing the appearance of its caption bar as if the window were changing from inactive to active status, or vice versa. (An inactive caption bar changes to an active caption bar; an active caption bar changes to an inactive caption bar.) Typically, a window is flashed to inform the user that the window requires attention but that it does not currently have the keyboard focus. The FlashWindow function flashes the window only once; for repeated flashing, the application should create a system timer. Please use the main window from FileMaker, not the window of solution.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.description: If this parameter is 1, the window is flashed from one state to the other. If it is 0, the window is returned to its original state (either active or inactive). When an application is minimized and this parameter is 1, the taskbar window button flashes active/inactive. If it is 0, the taskbar window button flashes inactive, meaning that it does not change colors. It flashes, as if it were being redrawn, but it does not provide the visual invert clue to the user.

examples.label: Flash Window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.FlashWindow"; "FileMaker Pro Advanced"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.GetAlpha
functions.description: Queries the current alpha value of the window. The alpha value is a number between 0.0 (invisible) to 1.0 (opaque).

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.GetClassName
functions.description: Queries the internal class name for the window. Sometimes it's easier to identify a window by the class name than the title. The title may change or be localized, but the class name typically stays the same. Added Mac support in Plugin version 3.3.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.GetCurrentTag
functions.description: Queries window tag for current window. The MBS Plugin manages a list of open windows, so you can query tag value for current window.

functions.name: Window.GetHeight
functions.description: Queries height of a window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.GetLeft
functions.description: Queries X coordinate of a window. See also Window.GetTop , Window.GetWidth and Window.GetHeight .

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

examples.label: Get position of front window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.GetLeft"; 0 ) & "/" & MBS( "Window.GetTop"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Figure out on which screen the first window is:
examples.text_code:
Let([
WindowCenterX =
MBS
( "Window.GetLeft"; 0 ) +
MBS
( "
Window.GetWidth
"; 0 );
WindowCenterY =
MBS
( "
Window.GetTop
"; 0 ) +
MBS
( "
Window.GetHeight
"; 0 );
$ScreenIndex
= -1;
r = While (
[
c =
MBS
( "
Screen.Count
" );
i = 0
] ;
i < c ;
[
l =
MBS
( "
Screen.Left
"; i );
t =
MBS
( "
Screen.Top
"; i );
w =
MBS
( "
Screen.Width
"; i );
h =
MBS
( "
Screen.Height
"; i );
$ScreenIndex
= If(
(l ≤ WindowCenterX) AND
(t ≤ WindowCenterY) AND
(WindowCenterX ≤ l + w) AND
(WindowCenterY ≤ t + h) ; i;
$ScreenIndex
);
i = i + 1 ] ; 0 )
];
$ScreenIndex
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
	WindowCenterX = MBS( "Window.GetLeft"; 0 ) + MBS( "Window.GetWidth"; 0 );
	WindowCenterY = MBS( "Window.GetTop"; 0 ) + MBS( "Window.GetHeight"; 0 );
	$ScreenIndex = -1;

	r = While ( 
	[ 
		c = MBS( "Screen.Count" ); 
		i = 0 
	] ; 
	i < c ; 
	[ 
		l = MBS( "Screen.Left"; i );
		t = MBS( "Screen.Top"; i );
		w = MBS( "Screen.Width"; i );
		h = MBS( "Screen.Height"; i );
		$ScreenIndex = If( 
			(l ≤ WindowCenterX) AND 
			(t ≤ WindowCenterY) AND 
			(WindowCenterX ≤ l + w) AND 
			(WindowCenterY ≤ t + h) ; i; $ScreenIndex ); 
		i = i + 1 ] ; 0 )

]; $ScreenIndex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: WindowRef

examples.label: Query minimum width for current window:
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: Window.GetTitle
functions.description: Get's the title of the window designated by the WindowRef. The WindowRef is returned by either Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex . Although it works on both platforms it was added specifically for Windows, where it can be used to get the the Name of the Application Window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

examples.label: Query title of front window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.GetTitle"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find names of all visible windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While(
[
	// query window list
	list = MBS( "Window.List" );
	titles = ""
];
// loop over window list
ValueCount(list ) > 1;
[
	window = GetValue(list; 1);
	visible = MBS("Window.IsVisible"; window);
	title = MBS("Window.GetTitle"; window);
	titles = If(visible; titles & title & ¶; titles);
	// next
	list = MiddleValues ( list ; 2; ValueCount(list )-1 )
]; titles )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.List
functions.description: Queries the list of FileMaker windows. See also RemoteControl.GetWindowsList function on Windows. We filter this list to have no windows of zero size and avoid having controls listed here on Windows.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass sum of the flags you like to use. 1: return names instead of IDs. 2: filter to only FileMaker document windows. 4: filter to only return visible windows.

functions.result: Returns the list of window references.

examples.label: Find all visible windows and return list of titles:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	list = MBS( "Window.List" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	WindowID = GetValue(list; index); 
	WindowVisible = MBS("Window.IsVisible"; WindowID);
	WindowTitle = MBS( "Window.GetTitle"; WindowID); 
	names = names & If(WindowVisible; WindowTitle & ¶; ""); 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the titles of visible windows
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.ModalDialogFront
functions.description: Checks if there is a front dialog window. This allows you to know in a trigger if a dialog window is currently open and maybe you better wait? This function is free to use. Created 29th October 2015 , last changed 22nd November 2015 Window.Minimize - Window.Move 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Window.Move
functions.signature: MBS( "Window.Move"; WindowRef; X; Y { ; NoAnimation; Duration } )
functions.description: Moves the window animated. On macOS we use Apples functions for animated move. On Windows we have our own code.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The new X position of the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The new Y position of the window.

parameters.name: NoAnimation
parameters.description: Whether to avoid animation. Pass 1 to disable animation. Default is 0 to allow animation.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: The duration in seconds. Can be a value between 0.1 and 5.0.

examples.label: Moves a window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.Move"; $WindowRef; 300; 200)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.Parent
functions.description: Queries the reference number of parent window. Can be used to query parent for a card window or dialog.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

examples.label: Try this in a card window:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
pw =
MBS
("Window.Parent"; 0);
ct =
MBS
("
Window.GetTop
"; 0);
wt =
MBS
("
Window.GetTop
"; pw);
cn =
MBS
("
Window.GetTitle
"; 0);
wn =
MBS
("
Window.GetTitle
"; pw)
]; cn & ¶ & ct & ¶ & wn & ¶ & wt )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
pw = MBS("Window.Parent"; 0); 
ct = MBS("Window.GetTop"; 0);
wt = MBS("Window.GetTop"; pw);
cn = MBS("Window.GetTitle"; 0);
wn = MBS("Window.GetTitle"; pw)

]; cn & ¶ & ct & ¶ & wn & ¶ & wt )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.PositionNextDialog
functions.signature: MBS( "Window.PositionNextDialog"; X; Y { ; Width; Height; Flags } )
functions.description: Sets location for next dialog. MBS Plugin watches for new windows to show and resize/move the next dialog. The dialog may briefly show before we can move it. There is the possibility you position a dialog outside visible screen area, so please check screen size yourself. To clear the settings, you can call function without parameters or with empty values.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The X position in points to use for the dialog. Can be empty.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The Y position in points to use for the dialog. Can be empty.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width in points to use for the dialog. Can be empty. We check against min/max size for dialog.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height in points to use for the dialog. Can be empty. We check against min/max size for dialog.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Whether to center dialog. Pass 1 to center on screen or 0 to not center. Pass 2 to center in front of the frontmost FileMaker document window.

examples.label: Shows dialog at specific location:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Window.PositionNextDialog"; 300; 300; 500; 400) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Test" ; "Hello World" ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.PositionNextDialog"; 300; 300; 500; 400) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Test" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Hello World" </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Control where progress dialog will show:
examples.text_code:
# define where the progress dialog should show.
# We can leave width and height empty to not change it
# move it to bottom of screen.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Window.PositionNextDialog"; 10; Get(ScreenHeight) - 270; ""; "") ]
# run the request
Insert from URL [ Select ; With dialog: Off ; Target:
Test::Result
;
$URL
; Do not automatically encode URL ]
# clear it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Window.PositionNextDialog") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> define where the progress dialog should show.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> We can leave width and height empty to not change it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> move it to bottom of screen.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.PositionNextDialog"; 10; Get(ScreenHeight) - 270; ""; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run the request</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="160" name="Insert from URL">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <DontEncodeURL state="True"/>
    <SelectAll state="True"/>
    <DisableStepCollapsed state="False"/>
    <VerifySSLCertificates state="False"/>
    <Calculation>3</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> clear it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.PositionNextDialog") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.RemoveMenu
functions.description: Removes the menu on a window. You can use this function to remove the FileMaker menu bar.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Can be 1 to remove menu entries instead of whole menu. That may be more permanent and allows you to get back invisible menu when you switch to layout mode and back. Default value 0 unassigns menu from Window.

examples.label: Remove menu:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.RemoveMenu"; $h)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>

functions.name: Window.Resizable
functions.description: Queries whether window is resizable.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.result: Returns 1 if window is resizable, else 0.

functions.name: Window.Resize
functions.signature: MBS( "Window.Resize"; WindowRef; Width; Height { ; NoAnimation; Duration } )
functions.description: Resizes the window animated. On macOS we use Apples functions for animated resizing. On Windows we have our own code. Please make sure on Windows that the window is not maximized as resizing a maximized window puts it in a mode where you can't manual resize it.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The new width of the window.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The new height of the window.

parameters.name: NoAnimation
parameters.description: Whether to avoid animation. Pass 1 to disable animation. Default is 0 to allow animation.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: The duration in seconds. Can be a value between 0.1 and 5.0.

examples.label: Resizes a window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.Resize"; $WindowRef; 300; 200)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Resize main window on windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.RunCommand

Be aware that this is not supported by FileMaker. Some commands work correctly, but others do nothing or can crash FileMaker.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The name of the command.

functions.name: Window.Screenshot
functions.signature: MBS( "Window.Screenshot"; WindowRef { ; MarginLeft; MarginTop; MarginRight; MarginBottom; ImageType; FileName } )
functions.description: Creates screenshot from a window. Returns the image as container value, so you can assign it to a container field or save to disk with Container.WriteFile function. Margins are different for Mac and Windows. Added iOS support in version 8.5, but margins are not supported there, so we always take full screen. On Windows may get wrong rectangle if Windows runs desktop with 200% resolution, but you have a 100% resolution screen. If you change zoom in Windows display controls while FileMaker runs, the plugin may not notice the change until you restart FileMaker.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: MarginLeft
parameters.description: The margin from left to cut away border.

parameters.name: MarginTop
parameters.description: The margin from top to cut away border.

parameters.name: MarginRight
parameters.description: The margin from right to cut away border.

parameters.name: MarginBottom
parameters.description: The margin from bottom to cut away border.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type. The image is not written to disk. This file name is just added to the container for later export.

examples.label: Creates screenshot:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.Screenshot"; Get(WindowName); Window Screenshot::Margin Left; Window Screenshot::Margin Top; Window Screenshot::Margin Right; Window Screenshot::Margin Bottom)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Screenshot of current window as JPEG:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.Scrollbar.GetScrollElasticity
functions.description: Queries the elasticity mode of the scrollbar in a FileMaker window. Value can be none, auto or allowed. Returns ? if something goes wrong or empty result if no scrollbar is found.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.Scrollbar.SetScrollElasticity
functions.description: Sets the scroll elasticity for a window. Since FileMaker 14 windows bounce on the scrolling edge and this function can change behavior.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The new mode to use. Can be auto, none or allowed.

examples.label: Sets the mode to none for front window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.Scrollbar.SetScrollElasticity"; 0; "none")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set scrollbar elasticity after delay of 0.1 seconds:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
";
0.1
; "
MBS
(\"Window.Scrollbar.SetScrollElasticity\"; 0; \"none\")") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 0.1; "MBS(\"Window.Scrollbar.SetScrollElasticity\"; 0; \"none\")") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.Scrollbar.getVerticalMaximum
functions.description: Queries maximum value for vertical scrollbar. Added Windows and iOS support for version 8.5. Looks like the maximum on Windows is always 65535.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.Scrollbar.getVerticalPosition
functions.description: Queries current value for vertical scrollbar. Added Windows and iOS support for version 8.5. Unit is points on macOS as distance from top. For Windows, the maximum is 65535, so the position is a fraction of that.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.Scrollbar.set
functions.description: Scrolls to the given coordinates. Added Windows and iOS support for version 8.5.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The new X position.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The new Y position.

examples.label: Scroll to middle of records on Windows:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$ListViewDisplayOffset
; Value: {half of the number of records visible on the screen in List Mode}]
Set Variable [
$$SetScrollbarPosition
; Value:
MBS
( "Window.Scrollbar.set"; "{Your Database Name}" ; 0 ; (65535/Get (TotalRecordCount)) * (Get(ActiveRecordNumber) -
$$ListViewDisplayOffset
))]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>{half of the number of records visible on the screen in List Mode}</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$ListViewDisplayOffset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.Scrollbar.set"; "{Your Database Name}" ; 0 ; (65535/Get (TotalRecordCount)) * (Get(ActiveRecordNumber) - $$ListViewDisplayOffset))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$SetScrollbarPosition</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.ScrollbarsVisible
functions.description: Whether scrollbars are visible or not in this window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.result: Returns 1 if scrollbars are visible or 0 if not.

functions.name: Window.SetAlpha
functions.description: Set Alpha can be used to set the transparency of a window. NOTE: If you set the Alpha of a window too low it will not accept mouse clicks. Windows limitation: Currently the plug-in can only set the transparency of Application Window, not the individual database windows. Seems not to work for FileMaker 11 on Windows with document windows. So please test if it works on your version.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.description: The new alpha value as a float value between 0.0 (invisible) to 1.0 (opaque).

examples.label: Set the Transparency of a window by name
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/

FileMakerApplicationWindowName =
Case(
Position(Get ( ApplicationVersion ); "Advanced"; 1; 1);"FileMaker Pro Advanced";
"FileMaker Pro"
);

WindowRef = 

MBS( 
"Window.FindByTitle";
FileMakerApplicationWindowName
);

Alpha = .5

];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/

MBS( 
"Window.SetAlpha";
WindowRef;
Alpha
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetBounds
functions.signature: MBS( "Window.SetBounds"; WindowRef; Left; Top; Width; Height { ; Animate } )
functions.description: Sets the bounds of the window. This is another way of resizing the window. On Windows this function can be used to adjust the size of the application window as well.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.description: The left bound of the window. If empty, leave that value unchanged.

parameters.name: Top
parameters.description: The top bound of the window. If empty, leave that value unchanged.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The new width of the window. If empty, leave that value unchanged.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The new height of the window. If empty, leave that value unchanged.

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Whether to animate transition on macOS. Pass 1 to animate or 0 to not.

examples.label: Set Window Bounds
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/
WindowRef = 0;
Left = 100;
Top = 100;
Right = 500;
Bottom = 500
];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/
MBS( 
"Window.SetBounds";
WindowRef;
Left;
Top;
Right;
Bottom
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Move debugger window:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetCloseButton
functions.description: Switches the close button on the window on or off. Please note! On Windows that SetCloseButton removes all buttons from the title bar and not just the close button. For runtime solution a special handling is needed. First on Mac you can simply use this function as normal to enable the close button and the window will close. On Windows simply enabling will not work as the close button is linked to a close menu entry in the system menu for this window. Earlier plugins had a script trigger, but that was non functional for years since Claris moved to Windows Presentation Foundation for window handling.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: State
parameters.description: The new state of the close button.

examples.label: Disable the Window Close button
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.SetCloseButton" ; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Enable the Window Close button
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.SetCloseButton" ; 0; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetCloseTrigger
functions.description: Sets close trigger. Informs you if a window with a reference closed or a custom web viewer was removed from the layout. FileMaker may rebuild the current layout and delete the custom web viewer. Parameter to the script is the reference number passed here to identify the object. Please be aware that Window objects are gone when the trigger runs.

parameters.name: Ref
parameters.description: Pass window or web viewer reference number.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Create web viewer and set close trigger:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$web
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.CreateWithControl
"; 0; "placeholder"; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Window.SetCloseTrigger";
$$web
; Get(FileName); "Closed") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "placeholder"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$web</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.SetCloseTrigger"; $$web; Get(FileName); "Closed") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetCurrentTag

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: Window.SetDockImage
functions.description: Sets the dock image for a window. The miniaturized window image is the image displayed in the Dock when the window is minimized. If you did not assign a custom image to the window, the value of this property is empty. When the user minimizes the window, the Dock displays this image in the corresponding Dock tile, scaling it as needed to fit in the tile. If you do not specify a custom image using this property, the Dock creates one for you automatically. You can also set this property as needed to change the minimized window image. Typically, you would specify a custom image immediately prior to a window being minimized. You can set this image while the window is minimized to update the current image in the Dock. However, you should not use this property to create complex animations in the Dock. Support for custom images is disabled by default. To enable support the plugin automatically sets the AppleDockIconEnabled key to true when updating the image. Due to this enable process, the function may only work the second time you call it after restarting FileMaker.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image to use. Can be container in various formats, e.g. PNG. If empty, clears existing picture.

examples.label: Set image for current window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.SetDockImage"; 0; Assets::Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Sets tag for window. The MBS Plugin manages a list of open windows, so you can set tag value for a window. Please be aware that referencing windows just by name is not good as window names may not be unique. Use Window.Current to get ID for current window. parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The new tag value. Empty text if not provided.

functions.name: Window.SetTitle
functions.description: Set the title of the window designated by the WindowRef. The WindowRef is returned by either Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex . Although it works on both platforms it was added specifically for Windows, where it can be used to Change the Name of the Application Window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title

examples.label: Set the Title of the Application Window
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.SetTitle" ; $$WindowRef; "New Title")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetTitleIcon
functions.description: Sets the icon of a window in Windows. Version 6.2 no longer takes a mask image like previous versions, so you now pass a picture with transparency like a PNG file for example. You may need to use GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer to read image from container first.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ImageRef
parameters.description: The image reference for the picture image, a number returned from the GMImage.New functions

parameters.name: bigIcon
parameters.description: Pass 1 to set big icon (for app switcher) or 0 for small icon (icon in title bar). Default is 0.

examples.label: Set title icon:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$imageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
test::Icon
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Window.SetTitleIcon"; 0;
$ImageRef
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; test::Icon) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Window.SetTitleIcon"; 0; $ImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetTitleVisible
functions.description: Sets whether title bar of window is visible. Set to 1 to make it visible and 0 to hide it. Requires OS X 10.10 or newer.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new visible setting.

examples.label: Hide titlebar

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetTitlebarSeparatorStyle
functions.description: Sets titlebar separator style. The type of separator that the app displays between the title bar and content of a window. Requires macOS 11.0 or newer.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The new mode. Value can be Automatic, None, Line or Shadow.

examples.label: Set titlebar separator:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetToolbarConfiguration
functions.description: Sets the toolbar configuration. Pass a xml with the toolbar's configuration information. If you want to provide a custom xml, you should first get the receiver's current configuration xml, then create a modified copy, rather than trying to construct one yourself. Do not depend on any details of the normal contents of a configuration dictionary. This may change with an update to macOS or FileMaker.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ConfigurationXML
parameters.description: The configuration XML. Should be the one returned by Window.GetToolbarConfiguration .

functions.name: Window.SetToolbarDisplayMode
functions.description: Sets mode of Mac toolbar. Default in FileMaker is IconAndLabel.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The new mode. Value can be Default, IconAndLabel, IconOnly or LabelOnly.

examples.label: Set to show icons and labels:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.SetToolbarVisible

parameters.name: WindowRef

functions.name: Window.SetTopMost
functions.description: Sets window to be topmost. If window is topmost, it floats over all other windows, like always on top.

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: Window.SetWindowDisplayAffinity
functions.description: Sets the window display affinity. Specifies where the content of the window can be displayed. The display affinity setting that specifies where the content of the window can be displayed. This parameter can be one of the following values:

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The display affinity setting that specifies where the content of the window can be displayed.

functions.name: Window.SetZoomButton
functions.description: This function enables or disables the the Zoom Button on a window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: On or Off
parameters.description: The new setting.

examples.label: Set The Zoom button

];

MBS(
"Window.SetZoomButton";
windowRef;
0
)

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Window.Show
functions.description: Shows the window Specified by the Window Reference. This can be used to show a window without bringing the window to the front.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

examples.label: Show a Window without bringing it the front

[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/

];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/
MBS( 
"Window.Show";
WindowRef
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.ShowTableViewDialog
functions.description: Shows the dialog to modify the fields for table view. Please run as last command in script, so the function can do the job properly. On Windows you can try similar by simulating a mouse click on the right coordinate.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Pass 1 to avoid checks whether button can be clicked. Normally plugin wants to avoid that you click there when not appropriate. But to toggle the button for a card window, you may want to force it as FileMaker does not support that.

examples.label: Show table view dialog command:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Window.ShowTableViewDialog"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Window.ShowsToolbarButton
functions.description: Queries whether toolbar on/off button should be shown or not.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.result: Returns 0 if toolbar button is hidden or 1 if toolbar button is visible.

functions.name: Window.ToggleFullScreen
functions.description: Toggles fullscreen mode for this window. If an application supports fullscreen, it should add a menu item to the View menu with toggleFullScreen as the action. Available in macOS 10.7 and later. This method does not much if you don't mark a window to be the primary fullscreen window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Window.TopHeight
functions.description: Queries window's toolbar height. This is the height for any toolbars between content and the window top border. On MacOS without frame, the top height is for example about 50 pixels for two toolbars in the window. With Frame, you include 81 pixels for toolbar and window title. So top height can be 131 as an example. Ruler is not included in that height, as it's part of FileMaker's content.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: WithFrame
parameters.description: Whether to include frame on Mac. Pass 1 to include frame. Default is 1 to include it.

functions.name: Window.Update
functions.description: Asks the window to redraw. This does not cause a FileMaker window to load new or modified records. It simply asks the window to redraw all controls.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire
functions.description: Starts the import process. May show dialogs to select images and progress dialog.

examples.label: Run acquire:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire") ]
#
If [ PatternCount (
$r
; "-536870363" ) = 1 ]
# no device with current ID, so clear and run again
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WinPhotoAcquire.SetCurrentDeviceID
"; "") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire") ]
End If
#
# now import what we got
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Import Images” ; Parameter:    ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> PatternCount ( $r; "-536870363" ) = 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> no device with current ID, so clear and run again</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.SetCurrentDeviceID"; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now import what we got</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Import Images"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.Files
functions.description: Queries list of files after acquire finished. You may need to delete those files after you imported them into FileMaker.

examples.label: Import images:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [ “PhotoAcquire” ; Animation: None ]
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "WinPhotoAcquire.Files") ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount(
$list
) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value: 1 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$ImagePath
; Value: GetValue(
$list
;
$i
) ]
If [
MBS
( "
Files.FileExists
";
$ImagePath
) ]
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$ImagePath
) ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
PhotoAcquire::Filename
;
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$ImagePath
) ]
Set Field [
PhotoAcquire::Image
;
$image
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
# Next
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="PhotoAcquire"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPhotoAcquire.Files") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($list; $i) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImagePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS( "Files.FileExists"; $ImagePath ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadFile"; $ImagePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $ImagePath ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="PhotoAcquire" id="99999" name="Filename "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$image </Calculation>
    <Field table="PhotoAcquire" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $i > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.GetCurrentDeviceID
functions.description: Queries device ID for currently selected devices. You can store this in a field to set back later with WinPhotoAcquire.SetCurrentDeviceID and pre-select this device for next run.

examples.label: Show identifier:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Device ID" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.GetCurrentDeviceID") </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.SetCurrentDeviceID
functions.description: Sets the current device.

parameters.name: DeviceID
parameters.description: the device identifier.

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.ShowSelectDeviceDialog
functions.description: Shows dialog to select device. This is called automatically within WinPhotoAcquire.Acquire if needed. We support Windows Portable Devices (WPD). Cameras (e.g. DSLR) and scanners of type Windows Image Acquisition (WIA). Devices of type Still Image Architecture (STI). Removable storage devices, such as CD drives or card readers. Digital video camera devices.

parameters.name: CPLMode
parameters.description: Run in control panel mode with additional controls to install devices. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Show select device dialog:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.ShowSelectDeviceDialog") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPhotoAcquire.ShowSettingsDialog
functions.description: Shows settings dialog. Settings are stored permanently into the registry.

examples.label: Show settings dialog:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinPhotoAcquire.ShowSettingsDialog") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
